build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors.

requirements.description: The player assigns reporters to story leads by dragging or clicking, with each reporter having skill ratings and specialties that affect story quality. Score 0 if no reporter assignment exists. Score 0.5 at most if reporters can be assigned but skills have no effect on outcomes. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A fact-check vs speed tradeoff exists: the player chooses between fast publication (higher error risk, captures readers) and thorough investigation (slower, more accurate). Score 0 if no speed/accuracy tradeoff exists. Score 0.5 at most if the choice is presented but has no effect on outcomes. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Published stories affect reputation (accuracy builds trust, errors damage it) and revenue (readership drives ad income and subscriptions). Score 0 if publishing has no consequence. Score 0.5 at most if reputation or revenue exists but is not affected by story quality. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The player arranges stories in the edition layout (front page, inside, buried) affecting their impact on readership and reputation. Score 0 if no layout mechanic exists. Score 0.5 at most if stories are published but placement has no effect. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Financial management requires balancing reporter salaries, equipment costs, and revenue to avoid bankruptcy, with visible budget tracking. Score 0 if no financial system exists. Score 0.5 at most if money is shown but has no effect on gameplay. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least 5 distinct story categories (politics, crime, sports, business, culture) with different complexity levels and reader interest. Score 0 if only one story type exists. Score 0.5 at most if categories differ only in label with identical mechanics. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 4 distinct reporter types with different specialties, reliability ratings, and salary demands. Score 0 if all reporters are identical. Score 0.5 at most if reporters differ only in name with identical stats. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Between editions the player can hire/fire reporters, invest in fact-checking tools, or expand coverage areas with at least 4 upgrade options. Score 0 if no between-edition management exists. Score 0.5 at most if options exist but produce no observable change. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The campaign spans 20+ editions with escalating story complexity, competitor pressure, and financial targets. Score 0 if difficulty never increases. Score 0.5 at most if editions pass but nothing changes. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

An edition summary shows stories published, accuracy rate, readership change, reputation change, and profit/loss. Score 0 if editions end abruptly with no summary. Score 0.5 at most if only one metric is shown. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Normal startup shows a styled title screen with the game name, newsroom or printing-press themed imagery, and a Start control. Score 0 if the game starts directly in gameplay or uses raw default UI. Score 0.5 at most if a title exists but is sparse text on a blank background. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The flow connects title to editor desk to story assignment to edition layout to publication results without restarting the executable. Score 0 if major transitions are missing. Score 0.5 at most if transitions exist but break visually. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The editor view clearly shows story leads, reporter assignments, edition layout, reputation meter, and financial status in readable non-overlapping regions. Score 0 if the player cannot determine newspaper state. Score 0.5 at most if information is present but overlapping or unreadable. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Player actions produce visible feedback: assigning reporters shows them working, publishing shows the edition, errors trigger correction notices, reputation changes animate, and financial updates flash. Score 0 if actions happen silently. Score 0.5 at most if only one feedback type exists. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Art direction is coherent across title, newsroom, stories, reporters, editions, and menus with a consistent newspaper-office palette and style. Score 0 if mostly default controls or mismatched placeholders dominate. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Reporter portraits, story cards, edition layouts, office furniture, and UI elements use real illustrated art with distinct newspaper-themed designs. Score 0 if primary elements are unstyled rectangles. Score 0.5 at most if art exists but is inconsistent in style. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation brings the newsroom to life: reporters typing, stories developing progress bars, edition printing animation, reputation meter movement, and deadline countdown effects. Score 0 if all elements are static. Score 0.5 at most if only one element animates. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI panels use styled themed widgets with newspaper column layouts, typewriter fonts, or press-room styling rather than raw Godot defaults. Score 0 if all UI is default Label/Button. Score 0.5 at most if some panels are styled but others remain raw defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
